message:
I'll never forget my neighbor Sarah laughing at me last spring when I casually mentioned I had a battery-powered lantern, two flashlights, AND a hand-crank radio in my hall closet. 'Overkill much?' she teased.

Then our power went out for 18 hours during a storm. Sarah showed up at my door with her phone at 3% and looking a little frazzled. I handed her one of my flashlights and we sat on the porch with my lantern, keeping our phones alive with a portable battery pack I'd picked up months earlier. Her kids actually thought the whole thing was an adventure because we had light and could keep their tablets going.

When the power came back on, Sarah didn't laugh. She just said, 'Okay, I'm buying flashlights tomorrow.'

Honestly? Being the gloriously over-prepared person in your friend group isn't about doom-scrolling or anxiety. It's just about knowing that when the unexpected happens (and it always does), you've got this—and maybe your neighbors do too.

Do you keep emergency supplies on hand, or is that something you've been meaning to get around to?
